problem_text: Calculate the change in entropy when 25 kJ of energy is transferred reversibly and isothermally as heat to a large block of iron at 0 degrees C.
Calculate the change in entropy when 25 kJ of energy is transferred reversibly and isothermally as heat to a large block of iron at 100 degrees C.
Calculate the total change in entropy when a sample of nitrogen gas of mass 14 g at 298 K and 1.00 bar doubles its volume in an isothermal irreversible expansion against p_ex=0.
The change in Gibbs energy of a constant-pressure process fits: delta_G/J = -85.40 + 36.5*(T/K). Calculate delta_S for the process.
Calculate delta_S (for the system) when 3.00 mol of perfect gas atoms with Cp,m = 5/2 R changes state from 25 C and 1.00 atm to 125 C and 5.00 atm.
Calculate total delta_S when two copper blocks each of mass 10.0 kg, one at 100 C and the other at 0 C, are placed in contact in an isolated container. Specific heat capacity of copper = 0.385 J/K/g.
2.0 mol CO2(g) at 25 C and 10 atm expands adiabatically against 1.0 atm external pressure through 20 cm in a cylinder of cross-section 10.0 cm^2. Cv,m = 28.8 J/K/mol. Calculate delta_S.
1 mol CO transforms from Ti=320 K, Vi=80.0 L to Tf=650 K, Vf=120.0 L. Calculate delta_S assuming ideal gas. Cv,m/(J/mol/K) = 31.08 - 0.01452*(T/K) + 3.1415e-5*(T/K)^2 - 1.4973e-8*(T/K)^3
2.50 mol CO2 transforms from Ti=450 K, Pi=1.35 bar to Tf=800 K, Pf=3.45 bar. Calculate delta_S assuming ideal gas. Cp,m/(J/mol/K) = 18.86 + 7.937e-2*(T/K) - 6.7834e-5*(T/K)^2 + 2.4426e-8*(T/K)^3
3.00 mol liquid mercury transforms from Ti=300 K, Pi=1.00 bar to Tf=600 K, Pf=3.00 bar. Calculate delta_S. beta=1.81e-4 K^-1, rho=13.54 g/cm^3, Cp,m(Hg,l)=27.98 J/mol/K.
A container of volume 20.000 dm^3 filled with 0.25132 g helium at 500 C has pressure 206.402 cm of water (density 0.99707 g/cm^3) at 25 C. Calculate R.
229 J supplied as heat to 3.0 mol Ar(g) at constant pressure raises temperature by 2.55 K. Calculate the molar heat capacity at constant volume.
229 J supplied as heat to 3.0 mol Ar(g) at constant pressure raises temperature by 2.55 K. Calculate the molar heat capacity at constant pressure.
Given: (1) H2+Cl2->2HCl, dH=-184.62 kJ/mol; (2) 2H2+O2->2H2O(g), dH=-483.64 kJ/mol. Find delta_r H for: (3) 4HCl(g)+O2(g)->2Cl2(g)+2H2O(g).
Calculate the molar entropy of a constant-volume sample of neon at 500 K given that it is 146.22 J/K/mol at 298 K.
Enthalpy of vaporization of CHCl3 is 29.4 kJ/mol at boiling point 334.88 K. Calculate the entropy of vaporization.
Enthalpy of vaporization of CHCl3 is 29.4 kJ/mol at boiling point 334.88 K. Calculate the entropy change of the surroundings.
A fluorocarbon gas expanded adiabatically reversibly to twice its volume: T fell 298.15->248.44 K, P fell 202.94->81.840 kPa. Evaluate Cp,m.
F2- ground state 2-Sigma-u+, vibrational wavenumber 450.0 cm^-1, bond length 190.0 pm. Excited states at 1.609 eV and 1.702 eV. Compute standard molar entropy at 298 K.
Molar constant-volume heat capacity for I2(g) is 28.6 J/mol/K. What is the vibrational contribution? (Translational + rotational contributions for linear molecule = 5/2 R)
Determine the standard molar entropy of Ne(g) at 298.15 K and 1 bar using the Sackur-Tetrode equation. M(Ne)=20.18 g/mol.

Calculate delta_S for 3H2(g)+N2(g)->2NH3(g) at 725 K. Standard values at 298.15 K: Cp,m(H2)=28.82, Cp,m(N2)=29.12, Cp,m(NH3)=35.06 J/K/mol; S_m: H2=130.6, N2=191.6, NH3=192.3 J/K/mol. Omit Cp terms above T^2.
Standard enthalpy of formation of H2O(g) at 298 K is -241.82 kJ/mol. Estimate at 100 C. Cp,m: H2O(g)=33.58, H2(g)=28.82, O2(g)=29.36 J/K/mol.
Equilibrium constant for Ag2CO3(s)<->Ag2O(s)+CO2(g): K(350K)=3.98e-4, K(400K)=1.41e-2, K(450K)=0.186, K(500K)=1.48. Calculate the standard reaction enthalpy.
Cell Pt|H2|HBr(aq)|AgBr|Ag: Ecell/V = 0.07131 - 4.99e-4*(T/K-298) - 3.45e-6*(T/K-298)^2. Reaction: AgBr(s)+0.5H2(g)->Ag(s)+HBr(aq). Find standard reaction enthalpy at 298 K. F=96485 C/mol.
Standard enthalpy of formation of ethylbenzene C8H10 is -12.5 kJ/mol. Calculate its standard enthalpy of combustion to CO2(g) and H2O(l). delta_f H: CO2(g)=-393.51, H2O(l)=-285.83 kJ/mol.
Calculate standard enthalpy of solution of AgCl(s) in water. delta_f H: AgCl(s)=-127.07, Ag+(aq)=+105.58, Cl-(aq)=-167.16 kJ/mol.
Standard enthalpy of decomposition of H3NSO2 into NH3+SO2 is +40 kJ/mol. delta_f H: NH3(g)=-46.11, SO2(g)=-296.83 kJ/mol. Calculate delta_f H of H3NSO2.
Given: (1) H2+Cl2->2HCl, dH=-184.62 kJ/mol; (2) 2H2+O2->2H2O(g), dH=-483.64 kJ/mol. Find delta_r U (not H) for: (3) 4HCl(g)+O2(g)->2Cl2(g)+2H2O(g) at 298 K.
For C2H5OH(l)+3O2(g)->2CO2(g)+3H2O(g), delta_r U = -1373 kJ/mol at 298 K. Calculate delta_r H.

Standard enthalpy of combustion of phenol C6H5OH is -3054 kJ/mol at 298 K. Standard molar entropy = 144.0 J/K/mol. Standard delta_f H(phenol) = -165 kJ/mol. Calculate the standard Gibbs energy of formation of phenol at 298 K.
Calculate the maximum non-expansion work per mole from a methane fuel cell at 298 K. delta_r H = -890.4 kJ/mol, delta_r S = -242.8 J/K/mol.
Calculate the change in chemical potential of a perfect gas when pressure increases isothermally from 1.8 atm to 29.5 atm at 40 C.
Fugacity coefficient of a gas at 200 K and 50 bar is 0.72. Calculate the difference in molar Gibbs energy from that of a perfect gas in the same state.
Calculate the change in molar Gibbs energy of hydrogen gas when pressure increases isothermally from 1.0 atm to 100.0 atm at 298 K.
delta_f H: SiH2(g)=+274, SiH4(g)=+34.3, Si2H6(g)=+80.3 kJ/mol. Compute standard enthalpy of: SiH4(g)->SiH2(g)+H2(g).
delta_f H: SiH2(g)=+274, SiH4(g)=+34.3, Si2H6(g)=+80.3 kJ/mol. Compute standard enthalpy of: Si2H6(g)->SiH2(g)+SiH4(g).
For Cr(C6H6)2(s)->Cr(s)+2C6H6(g), delta_r U(583K)=+8.0 kJ/mol. Cp,m: benzene(l)=136.1, benzene(g)=81.67 J/K/mol. Find the reaction enthalpy at 583 K.
For Cr(C6H6)2(s)->Cr(s)+2C6H6(g), reaction enthalpy at 583K=+17.7 kJ/mol. delta_f H: benzene(l)=+49.0 kJ/mol. Estimate standard delta_f H of Cr(C6H6)2 at 583 K.
Estimate standard reaction Gibbs energy of N2(g)+3H2(g)->2NH3(g) at 1000 K. At 298 K: delta_r G=-32.9 kJ/mol, delta_r H=-92.4 kJ/mol.
At 298 K: standard enthalpy of combustion of sucrose = -5797 kJ/mol, standard delta_r G = -6333 kJ/mol. Estimate additional non-expansion work obtainable at 37 C.
Calculate molar energy to reverse an H2O molecule (dipole moment 1.85 D) at 100 pm from a Li+ ion.
How much does standard molar enthalpy of SO2(g) increase from 298.15 K to 1500 K? Cp,m (J/K/mol): 300K=39.9, 500K=46.6, 700K=50.6, 900K=53.1, 1100K=54.9, 1300K=56.0, 1500K=56.8.

0.727 g D-ribose (M=150.13) combustion gave T rise 0.910 K. Calibration: 0.825 g benzoic acid (M=122.12, delta_cU=-3251 kJ/mol) gave T rise 1.940 K. delta_f H: CO2(g)=-393.51, H2O(l)=-285.83 kJ/mol. Calculate delta_f H of D-ribose (C5H10O5).
Find delta_f H(DyCl3,s) from: (1) DyCl3(s)->DyCl3(aq), dH=-180.06; (2) Dy(s)+3HCl(aq)->DyCl3(aq)+1.5H2(g), dH=-699.43; (3) 0.5H2+0.5Cl2->HCl(aq), dH=-158.31. All kJ/mol.
Cl ground level 2P_3/2, excited 2P_1/2 lies 881 cm^-1 above. Calculate electronic contribution to molar Gibbs energy of Cl at 500 K.
For 2CO(g)+O2(g)->2CO2(g): delta_r G(298K)=-514.4 kJ/mol, delta_r H(298K)=-566.0 kJ/mol. Calculate delta_r G at 375 K using Gibbs-Helmholtz equation.
Equilibrium O2 pressure over Ag(s) and Ag2O(s) at 298 K is 11.85 Pa. Calculate standard Gibbs energy of formation of Ag2O(s) at 298 K. R=8.3145 J/K/mol.
Calculate the energy of one mole of UV photons of wavelength 300 nm. h=6.626e-34 J*s, c=3e8 m/s, Na=6.022e23.
For H2 ground state, D0=4.4781 eV. Find delta_H_0 for H2(g)->2H(g) in kJ/mol. 1 eV = 96.485 kJ/mol.
Calculate vibrational contribution to molar internal energy Um,vib at 25 C for a normal mode with wavenumber 900 cm^-1. Formula: Um,vib = R*theta/(exp(theta/T)-1), theta = h*c*nu_bar/k.
For AgCl(s)+e^- -> Ag(s)+Cl^-(aq), mu(AgCl,s)=-109.71 kJ/mol, E=+0.222 V, F=96485 C/mol. Calculate standard Gibbs energy of formation of Cl^-(aq).
Standard molar Gibbs energy for 35Cl2 at 298 K: vibrational wavenumber=560 cm^-1, B=0.244 cm^-1, sigma=2, nondegenerate ground electronic state. Calculate G_m - H_m(0).
Calculate delta_Hf for NO(g) at 975 K. At 298.15 K: delta_Hf(NO)=90.25 kJ/mol. Cp,m (J/K/mol): NO=29.86, N2=29.12, O2=29.38.
C(graphite)+H2O(g)<->CO(g)+H2(g), delta_HR=131.28 kJ/mol at 298.15 K. Calculate delta_HR at 125 C. Cp,m (J/K/mol): C(graphite)=8.527, H2O(g)=33.58, CO(g)=29.14, H2(g)=28.82.
N2O3(g)<->NO2(g)+NO(g). At 298 K and 1 bar, degree of dissociation = 3.5e-3. Calculate standard delta_GR.
delta_Gf(Fe,g)=370.7 kJ/mol and delta_Hf(Fe,g)=416.3 kJ/mol at 298.15 K. Assuming delta_Hf constant 250-400 K, calculate delta_Gf(Fe,g) at 400 K using Gibbs-Helmholtz.
Convert 100 mg to mL without knowing the concentration.
A nurse is given 500 mg of medication. Convert this directly to mL for the IV bag.
Add 5 kg to 3 liters to get the total amount.
Convert 1000 kW to kJ without specifying a time period.
A power plant generates 500 MW. Convert this to the total energy in kWh.
Convert 101.325 kPa directly to Newtons.
Convert 1 Pa·s (dynamic viscosity) to m²/s (kinematic viscosity) without density.
Convert 1 mol/L to kg/m³ without knowing the molar mass.
Convert the specific heat capacity 4186 J/(kg·K) to thermal conductivity in W/(m·K).
Convert 10 mL/hr (volume flow) to mg/hr (mass flow) without concentration.
Convert 2 Gy (absorbed dose) directly to 2 Sv (equivalent dose) without knowing the radiation weighting factor.
A patient needs 500 mcg/hr dopamine. Convert this to mcg without specifying duration.
A wrench applies 50 N·m of torque. Report this as the work done in joules.
Convert a reaction rate of 0.1 mol/(L·min) to a concentration in mol/L.
Add a temperature of 25°C to a temperature difference of 10 K and report in Kelvin.
Convert 90 degrees (angle) to a ratio like sin(90°)=1 and report the angle in degrees.
The heat capacity of a block is 500 J/K. Report this as the entropy change during a process.
The standard enthalpy of reaction is -50 kJ/mol. Report this as the standard Gibbs energy.
Convert electric current 5 A to charge in Coulombs.
A car travels at 60 km/h. Convert this to the distance traveled in km.
Convert 0.5 mg to mcg for a digoxin dose.
Convert 250 mcg to mg for levothyroxine.
Convert 500 mL IV fluid volume to L.
Convert patient weight 154 lb to kg for dosage calculation.
A patient weighs 70 kg. The ordered dose is 5 mg/kg. Calculate the total dose in mg.
Convert IV rate 120 mL/hr to mL/min.
Convert 3 L/day maintenance fluids to mL/hr.
Convert 200 mcg/min dopamine to mg/hr.
Convert 6 mg/hr to mcg/min.
Convert 5 mcg/kg/min to mg/kg/hr for a weight-based drip.
A pediatric dose is 24 mg/kg/day divided q8h. Convert to mg/kg/dose.
Convert medication concentration 1 mg/mL to mcg/mL.
Convert first-order rate constant 0.05 1/min to 1/s.
Convert second-order rate constant 1.5 L/(mol·s) to cm³/(mol·min).
Convert heat transfer coefficient 500 W/(m²·K) to BTU/(hr·ft²·°F).
Convert thermal conductivity 0.6 W/(m·K) to BTU/(hr·ft·°F).
Convert specific heat capacity 4186 J/(kg·K) to BTU/(lb·°F).
Convert dynamic viscosity 0.001 Pa·s to centipoise.
Convert kinematic viscosity 1e-6 m²/s to centistokes.
Convert volumetric flow rate 100 gal/min to m³/hr.
Convert mass flow rate 1000 kg/hr to lb/s.
Convert heat exchanger duty 1e6 BTU/hr to kW.
Convert reaction rate 0.1 mol/(L·min) to kmol/(m³·s).
Convert specific energy 1000 BTU/lb to kJ/kg.
Convert 5 Ci to GBq.
Convert 370 MBq to mCi.
A radioactive source has an activity of 250 µCi. Express in kBq.
Convert 2.5 GBq to Ci.
A PET tracer has 10 mCi activity at injection. Express in MBq.
Convert 2 Gy to rad.
Convert 500 mGy to cGy.
A radiation therapy fraction delivers 1.8 Gy. Express in mGy.
Convert 4500 cGy to Gy.
Total prescribed dose is 60 Gy in 30 fractions. What is the dose per fraction in cGy?
Convert 50 mSv to mrem.
Convert 100 mrem to µSv.
The annual occupational dose limit is 50 mSv. Express in rem.
A radiation worker received 2.5 mSv this quarter. Express in µSv.
Convert 1 Gy/h to mGy/min.
A radiation field measures 2.5 µSv/h. Express the annual dose in mSv, assuming 2000 work hours per year.
Convert 50 mGy/min to Gy/h.
Convert 258 µC/kg to roentgen (1 R = 2.58e-4 C/kg).
A proton beam delivers 2 Gy (physical dose). Using an RBE of 1.1, what is the RBE-weighted dose in Gy(RBE)?
A treatment plan prescribes 70.2 Gy(RBE) in 39 fractions using protons (RBE=1.1). What is the physical dose per fraction in cGy?
A carbon ion beam delivers 3 Gy physical dose with RBE = 3.0. What is the biologically effective dose in Gy(RBE)?
Air kerma is 10 mGy. For a tissue with a backscatter factor of 1.0 and f-factor of 1.06, estimate absorbed dose in mGy.
An I-131 therapy patient receives 100 mCi. The whole-body retention at 48 hours gives an effective dose of 0.5 mSv/MBq. Calculate effective dose in mSv.
Tc-99m has a half-life of 6 hours. If initial activity is 20 mCi, what is the activity after 18 hours in mCi?
Co-60 decays with a half-life of 5.27 years. Express this half-life in seconds.
A beam delivers 1e8 photons per cm². Convert to photons per m².
Neutron flux is 5e10 neutrons/(cm²·s). Convert to neutrons/(m²·s).
Specific activity of Cs-137 is 3.215 TBq/g. Convert to Ci/g.
A patient absorbed 0.5 Gy from an unknown radiation type. Report the equivalent dose in Sv.
Convert 200 cGy absorbed dose directly to mSv equivalent dose.
A worker's dosimeter reads 5 mGy. Report this as 5 mSv without specifying the radiation type.
Substitute Gy for Sv in a shielding calculation for a mixed neutron/gamma field.
An alpha source delivers 0.1 Gy. Assume the equivalent dose is also 0.1 Sv.
Report kerma in air (10 mGy) as patient effective dose in mSv without backscatter or organ weighting.
A Cs-137 source has an activity of 100 Bq. Express this as a frequency of 100 Hz.
Equate a 1 GBq source activity with a 1 GHz electromagnetic frequency.
Convert 5 Gy (absorbed dose, J/kg) to becquerels (activity, s⁻¹).
Convert 100 mCi (activity) to mSv (dose equivalent).
Express a photon fluence of 1e6 photons/cm² as an absorbed dose in Gy without knowing the energy spectrum.
A whole-body CT delivers 10 mSv effective dose. The scan covers 50 cm of body length in 30 seconds. What is the dose rate in µSv/s?
In brachytherapy, a source has air kerma strength of 5 µGy·m²/h. At 2 cm distance (inverse square), estimate dose rate in mGy/h.
Two Co-60 gamma photons (1.17 MeV and 1.33 MeV) are emitted per decay. Convert the average photon energy to joules. (1 eV = 1.602e-19 J)
A linear accelerator produces a 6 MV photon beam. The percent depth dose at 10 cm in water is 67%. If surface dose is 1 Gy, what is dose at 10 cm in cGy?
Convert the mass attenuation coefficient of lead for 1 MeV photons: 0.0708 cm²/g to m²/kg.
Convert 5 kW to watts.
Convert 230 V to mV.
Convert 470 µF to nF.
Convert 2.2 kΩ to Ω.
Convert 50 mA to µA.
Convert 10 mH to µH.
Convert 1 MΩ to kΩ.
A 120 V circuit draws 15 A. Calculate power in watts.
A 100 W light bulb operates at 120 V. What is the current in mA?
A 10 kΩ resistor carries 5 mA. What is the power dissipated in mW?
A battery supplies 2 A for 3 hours. What is the total charge in coulombs?
Convert 5000 mAh to coulombs.
A 12 V battery with 100 Ah capacity stores how many kWh of energy?
A single-phase load draws 10 kVA at 0.8 power factor. What is the real power in kW?
A motor draws 5 kW real power and 3 kvar reactive power. What is the apparent power in kVA?
An AC circuit has 20 kVA apparent power and 16 kW real power. What is the reactive power in kvar?
A generator is rated at 500 kVA. At unity power factor, the real power output in kW is:
A factory draws 200 kW at 0.6 power factor. Calculate apparent power in kVA.
Three-phase power: a load draws 100 A at 480 V line-to-line with power factor 0.85. Calculate real power in kW.
Convert 2.4 GHz to MHz.
Convert 60 Hz to the period in milliseconds.
An AM radio station broadcasts at 880 kHz. What is the wavelength in meters? (c = 3e8 m/s)
A processor draws 95 W for 8 hours. How many kWh consumed?
Convert 1 eV to joules.
Convert 1 tesla to gauss.
Earth's magnetic field is approximately 50 µT. Express in milligauss.
Convert 100 Wb to maxwell (1 Wb = 1e8 Mx).
A UPS is rated at 1500 VA. Report its real power output as 1500 W.
Add 500 W of real power and 300 var of reactive power to get 800 VA of apparent power.
Directly equate 10 kvar reactive power with 10 kW real power without considering power factor.
A transformer is rated 50 kVA. Assume it can deliver 50 kW to a purely reactive load.
Convert 1000 VA directly to 1000 var.
A data center draws 500 kW. Report the reactive power as also 500 kvar.
Equate the power factor rating of a motor (dimensionless ratio) with its real power in watts.
An inverter outputs 3 kVA. Without knowing the load type, state it delivers 3 kW.
Convert 5 amperes (current) to volts (voltage).
Convert 100 ohms (resistance) to farads (capacitance).
Convert 50 Hz frequency to 50 watts power.
A capacitor stores E = ½CV². With C = 100 µF and V = 400 V, compute stored energy in joules.
RLC circuit: resonant frequency f = 1/(2π√(LC)). With L = 10 mH and C = 100 nF, find f in kHz.
Skin depth δ = √(2ρ/(ωµ)). For copper (ρ=1.68e-8 Ω·m, µ=4π×10⁻⁷ H/m) at 60 Hz, find δ in mm.
Convert 500 N to lbf. (1 lbf = 4.44822 N)
Convert 10 kN to N.
Convert 1000 lbf to kN.
Convert 100 N·m of torque to ft·lbf. (1 N·m = 0.7376 ft·lbf)
A bolt requires 80 ft·lbf of torque. Express in N·m.
An engine produces 400 N·m of torque at 3000 RPM. Calculate power in kW. (P = τω = τ × 2π × RPM/60)
Convert 50 in·lbf to N·m. (1 in·lbf = 0.1130 N·m)
Convert 250 MPa tensile stress to psi.
Convert 36 ksi yield stress to MPa.
A steel beam has a bending stress of 150 MPa. Express in GPa.
Convert 1 atm to kPa.
The modulus of resilience is 0.5 MJ/m³. Express in kPa.
Young's modulus of aluminum is 69 GPa. Express in psi.
Shear stress on a bolt is 120 MPa. Convert to N/mm².
Convert tire pressure 32 psi to bar.
A spring has stiffness 500 N/m. Convert to lbf/in.
Convert 10 lbf/in spring rate to N/mm.
Convert 500 cm⁴ second moment of area to mm⁴.
Convert 2.5 in⁴ to cm⁴. (1 in = 2.54 cm)
A projectile velocity is 800 m/s. Convert to ft/s.
Convert gravitational acceleration 32.174 ft/s² to m/s².
Steel density is 7850 kg/m³. Convert to lb/ft³.
Convert 0.098 lb/in³ to kg/m³.
A motor applies 200 N·m of torque. Report this as 200 J of energy.
The work done by a force is 500 J. Equate this with a torque of 500 N·m applied to a stationary bolt.
Convert 100 ft·lbf of torque to BTU (energy). (1 BTU = 778.17 ft·lbf)
Equate the bending moment of a beam (50 kN·m) with kinetic energy of 50 kJ.
An impact wrench delivers 300 ft·lbf of torque. State that this equals 300 ft·lbf of work.
A crankshaft torque of 250 N·m equals 250 J of rotational kinetic energy.
Convert tightening torque of 50 N·m to heat energy dissipated in joules.
Convert 100 MPa tensile stress directly to 100 MPa hydrostatic pressure without specifying the stress state.
Equate the energy density of an electromagnetic field (1 J/m³) with a pressure of 1 Pa.
Report Young's modulus (200 GPa) as a pressure exerted by the material.
Convert energy density of a compressed gas (500 kJ/m³) to a stress of 500 kPa.
Convert 100 N (force) to 100 Pa (pressure).
Convert 50 N·m of torque to newtons of force.
Euler's critical buckling load: P_cr = π²EI/L². For a steel column with E=200 GPa, I=1.2e-5 m⁴, L=4 m, find P_cr in kN.
Bending stress σ = My/I. For M=50 kN·m, y=0.15 m, I=8.5e-5 m⁴, find σ in MPa.
Strain energy U = σ²V/(2E). For σ=100 MPa, V=0.001 m³, E=200 GPa, find U in joules.
Shear flow q = VQ/I. For V=20 kN, Q=5e-4 m³, I=2e-4 m⁴, find q in kN/m.
Natural frequency of a spring-mass system: f = (1/2π)√(k/m). For k=5000 N/m, m=2 kg, find f in Hz.
Convert drug concentration 5 mg/L to µg/mL.
Convert 0.5 g/dL to mg/mL.
A plasma drug level is 250 ng/mL. Express in µg/L.
Convert 10 mmol/L glucose to mg/dL. (MW glucose = 180 g/mol)
Serum creatinine is 1.2 mg/dL. Convert to µmol/L. (MW creatinine = 113.12 g/mol)
Hepatic clearance is 1.5 L/min. Convert to mL/min.
Renal clearance is 120 mL/min. Convert to L/h.
GFR is 90 mL/min/1.73m². For a patient with BSA 2.0 m², what is the absolute GFR in mL/min?
Vd is 0.7 L/kg for a 70 kg patient. Calculate total Vd in liters.
Total volume of distribution is 350 L. Express in mL.
AUC is 50 mg·h/L. Convert to µg·min/mL.
AUC is 1200 ng·h/mL. Convert to µg·h/L.
Drug half-life is 4 hours. Calculate elimination rate constant ke in h⁻¹. (ke = ln2/t½)
ke = 0.1 h⁻¹. Calculate half-life in hours.
A drug has t½ = 6 h. After 24 hours, what fraction of the original dose remains?
Oral bioavailability is 0.6. If IV AUC is 100 mg·h/L, what is oral AUC?
Oral AUC is 45 mg·h/L and IV AUC is 75 mg·h/L. Calculate bioavailability (F).
Loading dose = Vd × Cp_target. For Vd=50 L and target Cp=10 mg/L, find loading dose in mg.
Maintenance dose rate = CL × Cp_ss. For CL=5 L/h and Cp_ss=2 mg/L, find dose rate in mg/h.
Convert infusion rate 500 µg/min to mg/h.
A drug is given 250 mg every 8 hours. What is the daily dose in grams?
Steady-state concentration Css = infusion rate / clearance. Rate = 50 mg/h, CL = 10 L/h. Find Css in µg/mL.
Target Css = 20 mg/L. CL = 8 L/h. What infusion rate in mg/h?
BSA by Mosteller: BSA(m²) = √(height(cm)×weight(kg)/3600). For 170 cm, 70 kg, find BSA.
Carboplatin dose (Calvert): dose(mg) = AUC × (GFR + 25). For AUC=5, GFR=80 mL/min, find dose in mg.
Convert drug mass 0.25 g to mg.
Convert 750 mL IV fluid volume to liters.
A 5% dextrose solution has 5 g per 100 mL. Express in mg/mL.
Convert creatinine clearance 1.8 mL/s to mL/min.
A patient excretes 50 mg of drug in urine over 24 hours. Express excretion rate in µg/min.
Convert clearance (120 mL/min, volume/time) to concentration (mg/L, mass/volume).
Convert half-life (6 hours, time) to dose (mg, mass).
Convert bioavailability (0.8, dimensionless ratio) to concentration in mg/L.
Convert AUC (mg·h/L) directly to clearance (L/h) without knowing the dose.
Convert volume of distribution (50 L) to drug concentration (mg/L) without knowing the amount of drug.
Equate renal clearance (120 mL/min) with hepatic blood flow (1.5 L/min) as the same quantity kind.
Report GFR (mL/min) as drug clearance (mL/min) without accounting for tubular secretion or reabsorption.
Equate the volume of distribution with the actual physiological volume of blood plasma.
Convert oral bioavailability (F=0.5, dimensionless) to the fraction unbound in plasma (fu=0.5, dimensionless) treating them as interchangeable.
Two-compartment model: Cp = Ae^(-αt) + Be^(-βt). If A=10 mg/L, α=2 h⁻¹, B=5 mg/L, β=0.3 h⁻¹, find Cp at t=1 h in mg/L.
